100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 27, 1926, the world witnessed significant developments, including the ongoing tensions in the Middle East as British forces continued to face unrest in Palestine amid rising nationalist sentiments. In Europe, the economic fallout from the post-war era remained palpable, with strikes in various industries highlighting labor discontent. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition sparked renewed discussions in Congress, as calls for reform intensified.
